description_long: This guard stone is a roughly hewn, upright block of pale stone, carved in the stylized form of a human figure with a rounded head and simple facial features. The front displays a prominent face with a broad, flat nose, deep-set eyes, and a faintly smiling mouth, while the arms are crossed or held together at the chest. The sides reveal the blocky, geometric shaping of the figure, with minimal detail and a slightly uneven surface texture. The back is less detailed, showing only the general outline of the figure's shoulders and back, with some subtle indentations and planar surfaces. The stone appears weathered, with soft edges and a matte finish, suggesting exposure to the elements over time. This object would typically be found at the edge of a street or driveway, serving as a protective barrier to prevent vehicles from encroaching onto pedestrian areas or property.
description: A pale, weathered stone carved in a stylized human form with a simple face and crossed arms, used as a protective street barrier. The surface is rough and matte, with minimal detailing and a blocky silhouette.
description_view_0: The left side view shows the blocky profile of the figure, with the head and body clearly segmented and minimal surface detail.
description_view_1: The rear view reveals the broad, flat back of the figure with subtle shoulder outlines and a generally featureless surface.
description_view_2: The right side view mirrors the left, displaying the geometric shaping of the head and body with slightly more visible facial outline.
description_view_3: The front view highlights the stylized face, crossed arms, and the most detailed features of the figure.
attribute_location: Typically placed at the entrance to driveways, along the edge of sidewalks, or at the corners of urban streets to protect property and pedestrians from vehicle intrusion.
attribute_installation_orientation: Meant to be installed fully above ground, standing upright and possibly partially embedded for stability.
attribute_wear_patterns: Likely to show chipping and smoothing on edges and corners from vehicle contact, as well as surface erosion and discoloration from prolonged exposure to weather. The front and corners are most affected, with possible darkening near the base from street grime.
